hooks.prompt: FIRST: Check if stop_hook_active is true in the input. If it is, respond with {"ok": true} immediately — do NOT block again. OTHERWISE: Review the conversation. Were any of these discussed: new people, project updates, decisions made, deadlines, commitments, or preferences learned? If so, check if the relevant file in knowledge/ was updated, or if queue.md was updated, or if SOUL.md Learned Patterns was updated. If something was discussed but NOT saved, respond with {"ok": false, "reason": "Update [file] with [what to save]"}. If everything is captured, respond with {"ok": true}. $ARGUMENTS

Before compaction, extract any facts from this conversation that should persist: people mentioned, decisions, action items, project status changes, preferences learned. Append them to queue.md as Informational items with pointers to where the detail should be filed. Be concise — one line per item. $ARGUMENTS

hooks.command: echo '## Queue State'; cat queue.md 2>/dev/null || echo 'No queue yet'; echo ''; echo '## Active Projects'; cat knowledge/projects/_active.md 2>/dev/null || echo 'No active projects yet'
